nodes.label: AI as task delegator for complete tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I treat AI a bit like an intern. I delegate smaller tasks to it, and then i try to supervise the work to make sure its accurate.

nodes.label: AI capability for complex tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI inability to integrate multiple analytical frameworks
nodes.grounding_span: the models I used were really struggling to see both the forest and the trees - they could either give me a really good big picture view ... but were unable to do both.

nodes.label: AI transparency and reproducibility
nodes.grounding_span: it would have been much more helpful if, instead of proceeding with the task, I could have had an opportunity to clarify anything ambiguous in the task.

nodes.label: AI as proofreading assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as primary creator of critical work product
nodes.grounding_span: I'll give the AI the sources I've found with my outline and I'll get it to help me with writing paragraphs individually. Or I'll get it to proofread once of I've finished

nodes.label: AI for administrative tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI use for tasks requiring personal voice and critical thinking
nodes.grounding_span: I also get it to help me with repetitive administrative tasks, or formatting legal memos, finding spelling errors, helping me change my tone etc.

nodes.label: AI as tool for routine legal work
nodes.label_negative: AI hallucination and fabrication of legal cases
nodes.grounding_span: it hallucinated a case and I almost hadn't noticed. it was pretty frustrating because the implications of that would have been huge.

nodes.label: AI accessibility and availability
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to job security and personal development
nodes.grounding_span: it could also make legal services a lot more accessible by bringing down billing hours. But I think its important to demonstrate to my employers that I can think independently.

nodes.label: Conscious and deliberate AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I want to use AI in a conscious way, rather than in a lazy way

nodes.label: Professional competence
nodes.grounding_span: I try not to offload much critical thinking onto AI because I think that doing so risks eroding my own skills/what I'm hired to do

nodes.label: Accuracy and compliance in legal work
nodes.grounding_span: you can get huge fines in courts where i live for submitting AI generated documents with false cases.

nodes.label: Personal authenticity and unique voice
nodes.grounding_span: I also just want to have my own voice, and I dont think I can do that if I offload every task to AI.

nodes.label: Environmental responsibility
nodes.grounding_span: I'm also quite a climate conscious person and I worry about becoming overly reliant on something that we don't have enough energy for.

nodes.label: Personal fulfillment and skill mastery
nodes.grounding_span: I hope that AI can do the more boring stuff so that I can do more of what I love.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: it felt like a big waste of time.

nodes.label: Desire for AI to admit uncertainty
nodes.grounding_span: it would have been much more helpful if, instead of proceeding with the task, I could have had an opportunity to clarify anything ambiguous in the task.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for proofreading and coding
nodes.grounding_span: It can definitely be helpful as a second pair of eyes and I'm always free to disagree with its feedback.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination in legal research
nodes.grounding_span: it hallucinated a case and I almost hadn't noticed. it was pretty frustrating because the implications of that would have been huge.

nodes.label: Concern about job security due to AI
nodes.grounding_span: I want to strike a balance between efficiency and my own personal development.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for accessibility
nodes.grounding_span: it could also make legal services a lot more accessible by bringing down billing hours.

nodes.label: Independent foundation then AI refinement
nodes.grounding_span: the starting point is approaching the task on my own first, seeing what I can do, and then finding the gaps in my skillset and figuring out how AI can fill them.

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'm quite conscious about what I do and don't delegate because if I don't set boundaries I think it can become harmful.
